text: Home-based training can still cover squat, hinge, push, pull, and trunk patterns with dumbbells, bands, and bodyweight. Exercise selection should emphasize simple setup and stable positions. Single-leg work and floor-based pressing are often practical when heavier equipment is unavailable.
title: Evidence Brief - Home-Based Resistance Training

text: Moderate repetition ranges are useful because home loads may be limited. Progression can come from extra repetitions, slower tempo, or an additional set before a large load increase. Home plans should avoid depending on niche machines or highly technical lifts.
title: Evidence Brief - Home-Based Resistance Training

text: When the equipment list is narrow, it is better to repeat a few reliable movements than to invent unusual exercises. Substitutions should keep the same broad pattern and the same conservative intent.
title: Evidence Brief - Home-Based Resistance Training
